# Intro — What is a Robot? {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

## Quick Vote! {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

Is each of these a robot?

::: {.incremental}

- 🚗 A self-driving car — **Robot or Not?**
- 🦾 A factory arm that welds car parts — **Robot or Not?**
- 📱 A smartphone — **Robot or Not?**
- 🤖 The Keyestudio 4WD car you'll build today — **Robot or Not?**

:::

::: {.fragment}

> A robot is anything that **senses** its environment, **thinks** (runs code), and **acts** on the world.  
> Your 4WD car will do all three. 🎉

:::

## The Three Things Every Robot Does

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width="33%"}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}

## 👁️ Sense
Ultrasonic sensor, IR receiver, light sensor — the robot *feels* the world.

:::

::::

:::: {.column width="33%"}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}

## 🧠 Think
The Arduino runs your C++ code — it *decides* what to do.

:::

::::

:::: {.column width="33%"}

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}

## 💪 Act
Motors spin, LEDs light up, buzzers beep — the robot *does* something.

:::

::::

:::::

## But First — Why Electricity? {.sdaia-dark background-color="#1C355E"}

::: {.r-fit-text}

Everything in your robot runs on **electricity**.

:::

::: {.fragment}

To build a robot, you need to understand **one big idea**:

> Electricity is like water flowing through pipes.

:::

::: {.fragment}

Let's see how.

:::
